title: The Ocean Waits
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A couple moves into a coastal house where the tide never fully goes out and something in the shallows watches the windows at night.
long_description:
Nora and Paul inherit a house on a coastal inlet from Paul's great-aunt Eileen. The house is beautiful, isolated, and wrong. The tide never fully goes out. At night, a green light flickers beneath the surface. The neighbor issues warnings that arrive too late.

As Nora delves into Eileen's journal and the house's dark history, she uncovers a two-hundred-year-old bargain. Something ancient lives in the inlet. It has been waiting for company. And it has chosen Nora as the next keeper.

The Ocean Waits is a slow-burn horror novel about loneliness, patience, and the terrible beauty of being truly seen. Literary and spare, it builds dread through quiet observation rather than sudden shocks.

main_characters.name: Nora
main_characters.description: Wife, early thirties, an art conservator by training. Observant and patient. She is the one the water chooses.

main_characters.name: Paul
main_characters.description: Husband, mid-thirties, a freelance journalist. Rational, skeptical. He sees the danger before Nora does but cannot pull her away.

main_characters.name: Mrs. Collow
main_characters.description: The neighbor, an elderly woman who has lived on the inlet for sixty years. She knows the truth and tries to warn them.
